overview.what: A sentiment analysis tool that classifies text into positive, negative, or neutral categories without needing a dedicated training dataset. It leverages large pre-trained models for immediate zero-shot classification.
overview.how_it_works: The system uses the Natural Language Inference (NLI) approach, where the model evaluates how well a premise (the text) fits a hypothesis (e.g., 'This text is positive'). It returns a probability distribution across defined labels.

files.description: Zero-shot inference using the pipeline API.
files.code:
from transformers import pipeline

classifier = pipeline("zero-shot-classification", model="facebook/bart-large-mnli")

def classify_sentiment(text):
    candidate_labels = ["positive", "negative", "neutral"]
    res = classifier(text, candidate_labels)
    return res['labels'][0], res['scores'][0]

# Example
# label, score = classify_sentiment("This platform is amazing!")
# print(f"{label} ({score:.2f})")
